title: 'podman cp' copy between host and container
body:
fix #613  Copy files/folders between a container and the local filesystem
Signed-off-by: Qi Wang <qiwan@redhat.com>

comments.body: I think we need to make sure the cases listed in https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline/cp/ right after the line beginning with `Assuming a path separator` are properly handled
Also, I'd love more comments, the code is very hard to follow in places right now
`libpod/docs/podman-cp.1.md`, how to change the current description :neutral_face:
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #2184) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
/retest
@QiWang19 Containers/storage now has the copyarchive stuff, Please rework this to use those vendors.
@QiWang19 just make sure the vendor.conf in your sandbox has the same id for containers/storage as upstream does.  If not, you'll want to do a fetch/rebase.
I see the vendored changes, but no change to vendor.conf
@TomSweeneyRedHat  :ok_hand: need to change `master` to `v1.9`
LGTM other than some man page "we's".  Would like a @mheon head nod.
/retest
/retest
@mheon PTAL

I currently trying this nice feature and I observe that my files was not copied from or to my testing container:
```shell

error evaluating symlinks "": lstat /home/herve/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/df486e0ab8ab9782aae30ce09f181e715aab2981f0e5d1e1735625fcf6b88f07/merged/boum.txt: no such file or directory
```

I hope this test can be useful.

Just FYI (related to my previous message), if I inspect the storage overlay after the copy from the host to the container I can observe that my files is present inside the overlay but not inside the container:
```shell
$ ls /home/herve/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/df486e0ab8ab9782aae30ce09f181e715aab2981f0e5d1e1735625fcf6b88f07/merged 
README.md
```

thanks, working on this. but confusing, looks like I don't meet this issue  :confounded:

@mheon sorry I'm not sure... I'm a newcomer in this world how I can check this?
@QiWang19 sorry still to doesn't work on my side.

@4383 Rootless = running containers as a non-root user

`cp` right now doesn't properly handle the rootless user namespace, I think
It helps when I press the right button and don't accidentally close PRs

@mheon thanks for the explanations :)

So yeah I running containers as a non-root user
We should through an error right now when they run rootless until @giuseppe Fixes the issues.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #2274) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@QiWang19 Lets finish this up tomorrow, I am back in office.
LGTM
/lgtm
needs approve label to test tide?

Oh, nobody approved
/approve
